claim: For all integers n > 2, Euler's totient phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is odd, then phi(n) is divisible by 2.
predicate: n <= 2 or n % 2 == 1 or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a power of 2 greater than 4, then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (n > 4 and (n & (n-1)) == 0) or phi(n) % 4 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a power of 2 greater than 4 or an odd integer greater than 3, then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or (n % 2 == 1 and n > 3) or not (n > 4 and (n & (n-1)) == 0) or phi(n) % 4 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n has at least two distinct odd prime factors, then phi(n) is divisible by 8.
predicate: n <= 2 or len([p for p in range(3, int(n**0.5)+2, 2) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) < 2 or phi(n) % 8 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is divisible by an odd prime and is not a prime power, then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or (is_prime(n) and n > 2) or len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+2) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) == 1 or phi(n) % 4 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is divisible by 3 and by another prime, then phi(n) is divisible by 6.

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is prime, then n-1 is even.

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is prime and n > 3, then n-1 is divisible by 2 and by 3 or by 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or not is_prime(n) or n <= 3 or ((n - 1) % 2 == 0 and ((n - 1) % 3 == 0 or (n - 1) % 4 == 0))

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is prime and n ≡ 1 mod 4, then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or not is_prime(n) or (n % 4 != 1) or phi(n) % 4 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is prime and n ≡ 3 mod 4, then phi(n) is divisible by 2 but not necessarily by 4.
